GID
Assembly Guide Timeline Electrical Spec System Diagram Illustrated Spec Routing Map

Galactic ID Photobooth

Fabrication Progress Timeline
TSL (The Specialists LTD) × Apple Industries  |  December 2025 — April 2026
Compiled from email correspondence: Paul Ward ↔ Jesse Weisberg
PHASE 1 Initial Contact & Concept Development Dec 1–22, 2025
Dec 1 — First Contact

Jesse reaches out to TSL via contact form. Describes Apple Industries photo booth business and the goal: transform the PHOTOMA mini into a Star Wars Galactic ID machine. Target: 100+ units by end of March, timed for the Mandalorian film release.

Dec 5 — NDA & CAD Exchange

Countersigned NDA and PHOTOMA mini STEP file (PHM_Vendor.step) delivered. Lucasfilm meeting confirmed for Dec 11.

Dec 11 — Two Concept Renders Delivered

Paul delivers "Dropship" Concept (Imperial starship with deep vacuum-formed 3D panels) and "In-World Kiosk" Concept (shallower panels — "space self-service DMV"). Vacuum-formed exterior panels estimated at $300–$600/unit at 100-unit scale. Lucasfilm loved both; the kiosk inspired a "passport droid" idea.

Dec 22 — Design Direction Narrowing

Lucasfilm leaning toward something more commercial/minimal with a holographic projection display on top. Also considering R2-themed projector alternative.

PHASE 2 Design Direction & Full Proposal Jan 26 – Feb 12, 2026
Jan 26 — Lucasfilm Approves Design Direction

Jesse shares Lucasfilm-approved brief (StarWars_GalacticID_Cabinet_Presentation_20260125.pdf). Requests fabrication cost estimate. Timeline: 20–30 sets mid-March, 70–80 more in April.

Feb 12 — Full Proposal Delivered

Paul delivers working mockup model with overview drawing and render document. Complete kit scope and cost breakdown:

PhaseEstimate
Design$9,000–$13,000
Prototype / First Unit (3–4 weeks)$25,000–$35,000
Vacuum Mold Tooling (4 molds)$40,000–$55,000
Scale Production — 100 units w/ LEDs$5,000–$5,500/unit
Scale Production — 100 units w/o LEDs$4,000–$4,500/unit
Kit includes: 2× Pill Lighting Panels, 1× Speaker Cover, 1× Instrument Panel, 1× Display Cover with camera shroud, 2× Side ID Display Panels, 1× Print Chute Border, 4× Box & Hose Assemblies, 1× Hologram Fan Tower Housing.
PHASE 3 Design Review & Production Kickoff Mar 2–6, 2026
Mar 2 — Final Aesthetic Review Call

Paul sends 3D renders ahead of the design review meeting. Aesthetic direction finalized on the call.

Mar 6 — Project Approved & Deposit Paid

Jesse approves project and deposit invoice. STEP file of final model confirmed as a deliverable. TSL begins fabrication.

PHASE 4 Technical Design & Prototyping Mar 10–18, 2026
Mar 10 — Design Revision Document (Rev3)

Full set of updated renders delivered showing the refined design with all major kit components visible.

Updated full-booth renders — isometric, front, and rear views
Holo Tower — redesigned without negative space for cleaner vacuum-form trimming
Side Panel — sized over bump-out, 5 pill lights (down from 6), white backlights on IDs. First lighting test shown (on/off).
Panel Cutout Pattern — instrument panel: 3 rectangular cutouts on inner fascia; side panel cutout highlighted in blue
Side panel light diffusion — panel sits on existing sheet-metal bump-out as riser; most of bump-out cut out for diffusion
Mar 13 — Prototyping Questions

Short Box Design: Less space under unit than expected — working on fit. Camera Housing: Need to power on booth to check lens occlusion. Asked for access code (only received physical keys).

Mar 17 — Holo Tower Prototype & Paint Samples

Major prototyping milestone: 3D-printed PLA Holo Tower assembled and mounted on booth for the first time.

Holo Tower prototype — 3D printed PLA shells, assembled with clear cover and holographic fan
Holo Tower mounted in place on booth — first full-height integration (TSL shop floor)

Paint samples delivered:

Light Metal: Orion Silver w/ Satin Clear (speaker borders, chute border). Dark Metal: Dark Stainless Steel Silver w/ Satin Clear (hose boxes, holo tower). Next: Cloudy Dayz Metallic to be sampled.
Side Panel Lighting V2 — addressable RGB Neopixels, diffused through milk acrylic (left) and frosted clear acrylic (right). Red pills backlit with white LED strip.
Mar 18 — Plastic Cover & Continued Build

Original plastic cover mostly self-supporting; replacement being designed. Progress photos show elements being mounted to the booth.

PHASE 5 Alpha Unit Assembly & Materials Refinement Mar 20 – Apr 3, 2026
Mar 20 — New Email Thread

Thread restarted ("TSL x Apple Industries: Galactic ID Photobooth Alpha Production") — old thread caused Exchange bouncebacks due to size.

Mar 26 — Modem Mount & Rear Panel

Modem configuration confirmed — no issues reaching ports. Rear side panel removed per Jesse's direction, focusing on front-facing elements.

STEP file view — modem cable routing (blue) with antenna holes (red circles) on top panel of booth assembly
Mar 30 — Dark Metallic Sample 3

Two new dark metallic paint samples: one warmer/lighter, one colder/darker. Three-color palette clarified: Light Metallic, Dark Metallic, Black (left to right).

Mar 31 — Side Panel & Pill Light Integration

Major physical assembly progress documented:

Side Panel: black thumbscrews installed ("they look great!"). Pill lights backlit with white 12V COB LED strips (lenses already red).
Pill Light Panels — 12V Cool White LED strip installed. TSL notes it's too blue; auditioning warmer COB strip. Three views of full panel illumination.
Instrument Panel lit up — backlit LED tiles nearly flush with top plate, glued in. 4 LED bulbs on left being replaced. Blue/red tiles show faint outline of mounting lip.
Tile recess option: can sink tiles 1/16" deeper on production units to hide outline (similar to side panel pill lights). Side panel w/ sample IDs mounted.
Light Backer requirement: Inner door needs white vinyl skin for ideal pill light and side panel reflectivity. TSL used paper for prototype; white vinyl recommended for production.
Apr 1 — PSU Sizing Recommendation

Vendor-corrected power calculations reveal existing PSU is undersized. Paul recommends sizing up to a larger PSU.

Apr 2 — Instrument Panel Tile Recess

Paul confirms instrument panel tiles will be recessed under the main display. Called for confirmation before cutting; emailed for documentation.

Apr 3 — Dark Metallic Paint Round 4 & Wiring

New dark metallic paint arrived early. Samples 4A and 4B sprayed and photographed on-booth. Instrument panel wiring progress shown.

Dark Metallic samples: Top=4B, Bottom L→R: 4A, 3A, Light Metallic. Can split the difference between 4A and 4B.
Instrument Panel Wiring (WIP) — Wago connectors join all LEDs to one 12V connection; can undo without desoldering. 4 indicator lights still to wire.
Pill Light LEDs — strips stuck to back of wood along columns and perimeter. 2 strips per wall. Upper: unlit; Lower: illuminated.
Side Panel mounted — existing bump-out removed, riser plate added (free of charge). Acrylic template for mounting geometry. Two LED tails: 12V white + addressable RGB.
PHASE 6 Kit Production & STEP Delivery Apr 4–9, 2026
Apr 4 — Kit Production in Full Swing

Site visit postponed; rescheduled for following week. Paul notes kit production is in full swing with plenty to see.

Apr 6 — Visit Scheduled 4:30 PM+

Time set to allow newly painted parts to be installed and templates prepared for Jesse's review.

Apr 9 — STEP File Delivered

Paul delivers STEP file of photobooth assembly as currently modeled. May update further for final design. Jesse spots antenna hole sizing — Paul confirms 0.551" in model (pilot hole on template, not full-size).

Status as of April 10, 2026

  • Alpha unit assembly actively in progress at TSL's shop (Ridgewood, NY)
  • STEP file of current assembly delivered for Apple Industries engineering review
  • Paint palette in final selection — Orion Silver (light) + Dark Metallic 4A/4B (choosing between them) + Black
  • Kit production in full swing for first batch of 10 units
  • Site visit at TSL scheduled (4:30 PM+, week of Apr 6)
  • Open items: PSU sizing upgrade, final dark metallic shade selection, pill light LED color temp (warm vs cool white), antenna hole full-size vs pilot
